what_it_does: Defines shared React UI primitives for a Type Registry, including layer and provenance badges, inline type chips, type cards, lineage display, slot acceptance and fill views, search/layer filtering, and a nested type tree, then exposes them on window.
observations: Uses global React aliases for hooks and global registry/UI objects via window.CV_REGISTRY, CvIcon, and optional TypeThumb.
Encodes layer and provenance as small visual badges used across chips, cards, trees, and lineage rows.
TypeCard summarizes a Type with thumbnail, metadata, description, slots/children/variables/tags, and an optional Use action.
TypeSlotList renders declared slots, accepts hints, current filled values, and optional browse actions.
TypeTree groups query results by registry layer and nests children based on extends relationships within the filtered set.
The file exports by assigning component functions onto window rather than using module exports.
standouts: A small visual vocabulary for Type Registry concepts is concentrated in one file.
Registry-backed metadata display and selection callbacks are kept in reusable components.
Slot composition is represented both as accept constraints and current filled Type references.
conventions: Functional React components with destructured props and early null/empty returns.
Optional chaining is used for registry access and Type metadata.
CSS class names are prefixed with cv-type, cv-slot, or cv-prov for registry UI styling.
Components favor global runtime objects over explicit imports or injected dependencies.
Nested tree rendering is implemented recursively through TypeTreeNode.
concerns: LAYER_RANK is defined but unused in this file.
useState_tu is destructured from React but unused.
TypeSlotList accepts onChange but does not use it.
TypeChip renders a button without an explicit type attribute, which can matter inside forms.
Several buttons rely on symbolic text such as arrows or multiplication sign without aria-labels in this file.
notes: This file is the Type Registry UI primitive layer: badges, cards, slot views, filters, and tree navigation wired to the global registry.
questions: Should TypeSlotList support mutation through its onChange prop, or is it intentionally read-only here?
Is LAYER_RANK intended for ordering in this file, or retained for another consumer pattern?
Are accessibility labels supplied elsewhere for icon-only or symbolic controls?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: the file provides shared visual primitives used by registry consumer surfaces for coherent Type display and browsing.
intention_signals: The file seems to be trying to make Type Registry concepts consistently visible and selectable across different surfaces without repeating badge, card, slot, filter, or tree UI logic.
novelty: The distinct aspect is the combination of registry lineage, layer taxonomy, provenance, slots, thumbnails, and filtered tree browsing as lightweight global React primitives.
contribution: This file contributes a reusable UI substrate for presenting and navigating typed design-system records, including visual encodings for layer, provenance, lineage, slot compatibility, filled slot references, searchable filtering, and layer-grouped hierarchy.
summary_for_embedding: Shared React primitives for a Type Registry that render Type badges, chips, cards, lineage, slot constraints, filled slots, filters, and a nested layer-grouped tree from window.CV_REGISTRY.
intention_for_embedding: This was for giving registry consumer surfaces a coherent, reusable UI vocabulary for inspecting, selecting, and composing Types.

symbols.does: Renders a small layer badge for a Type layer.
symbols.description: Looks up layer metadata from window.CV_REGISTRY.LAYER_INFO, derives pip size from the size prop, and displays a colored pip plus label. Returns null when the layer is not known.

symbols.does: Renders a provenance badge for built-in, user, vi, or imported Types.
symbols.description: Maps a provenance key to a label and tone value, falling back to built-in. The rendered class includes the raw provenance key while the badge title uses the mapped label.

symbols.does: Renders a compact clickable Type chip.
symbols.description: Displays the Type icon, name, and layer badge inside a button. It supports an active class and calls the supplied onPick handler when clicked.

symbols.does: Renders a grid/card presentation for a Type.
symbols.description: Shows an optional generated thumbnail, layer/provenance overlay, icon, name, family, optional lineage marker, description, slot/child/variable/tag stats, and optional Use button. It reads child counts from the global registry and prevents the Use button click from triggering the card open handler.

symbols.does: Renders a Type lineage breadcrumb strip.
symbols.description: Fetches the lineage chain from window.CV_REGISTRY.lineage(id), reverses it for display, and renders each Type as a clickable breadcrumb with a layer badge and name. It returns null when no lineage is available.

symbols.does: Renders a textual summary of what a slot accepts.
symbols.description: Builds accept hints from slot.accepts.layers, families, and tags. If no accept constraints are present, it displays that any Type is accepted.

symbols.does: Renders a Type's slot definitions and current slot fills.
symbols.description: Iterates over type.slots and displays each slot label, key, multiple/optional flags, accept hint, current filled Type chips or empty state, and optional Browse Types action. If the Type has no slots, it renders an empty-state message about composability.

symbols.does: Renders a chip for a slot-filled Type id.
symbols.description: Looks up the Type by id from window.CV_REGISTRY. If found, it delegates to TypeChip; if missing, it renders the unresolved id in a missing fill span.

symbols.does: Renders search and layer filter controls for Type browsing.
symbols.description: Provides a search input with clear button and a layer segmented control. Changes are reported by calling onChange with an updated filter object while preserving other filter fields.

symbols.does: Renders a compact layer-grouped Type tree.
symbols.description: Queries the registry using the provided filter, groups results by layer, and renders each layer group with roots and recursively nested descendants. Root selection is scoped to the filtered set, and an empty state appears when no Types match.

symbols.does: Renders one recursive node in the Type tree.
symbols.description: Displays a Type as a selectable button with icon, name, and provenance badge. It computes children by matching extends against the current node id within the provided list, then recurses for nested children.
